title: Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette

seo.title: Korean Rolled Omelette (Gyeran Mari) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make delicious Korean Rolled Omelette (Gyeran Mari) with this easy, authentic recipe. Perfect for a savory breakfast or snack. Get step-by-step instructions!
longTailKeywords: how to make Korean rolled omelette
authentic Korean rolled omelette Gyeran Mari
Gyeran Mari step by step recipe
best Korean rolled omelette recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Korean Rolled Omelette (Gyeran Mari) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Korean Rolled Omelette (Gyeran Mari)?
How to Make Korean Rolled Omelette: Step-by-Step
Serving Suggestions for Your Korean Omelette
Frequently Asked Questions About Gyeran Mari
seo.imageAlt: Close-up of freshly sliced Korean Rolled Omelette (Gyeran Mari) on a plate.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this fluffy and savory Korean Rolled Omelette, known as Gyeran Mari, a delightful dish for any meal!

description: Gyeran Mari is a beloved Korean rolled omelette, a staple side dish or banchan known for its soft, fluffy texture and delicate flavor. This versatile dish is made by rolling thin layers of seasoned egg, often with finely chopped vegetables, creating an appealing spiral when sliced. It is a wonderfully comforting and nutritious addition to any Korean meal or a delightful breakfast on its own.

ingredients.notes: optional, for a hint of sweetness

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Finely dice the carrot and chop the green onions. Ensure all ingredients are ready before starting to cook, as the rolling process requires quick action.

instructions.title: Whisk the Eggs
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, crack the eggs. Add the finely diced carrot, chopped green onions, salt, and optional sugar. Whisk thoroughly until all ingredients are well combined and the egg mixture is smooth. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve for an extra smooth omelette, if desired.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a non-stick rectangular or round pan (about 8-10 inches) over low to medium-low heat. Lightly grease the pan with a thin layer of vegetable oil using a paper towel. Maintaining low heat is crucial for slowly cooking the omelette without browning.

instructions.title: Start Rolling
instructions.text: Pour about 1/4 to 1/3 of the egg mixture into the heated pan, tilting the pan to spread it thinly and evenly. Let it cook until the edges are set and the top is mostly cooked but still slightly wet. Begin to gently roll the egg from one end towards the center using a spatula or chopsticks, leaving about one-third of the cooked egg unrolled at the end.

instructions.title: Continue Rolling and Cooking
instructions.text: Push the partially rolled egg to one side of the pan. Lightly re-grease the empty part of the pan if needed. Pour another 1/4 to 1/3 of the egg mixture into the empty space, making sure some of it seeps underneath the rolled egg. Cook until set, then continue rolling the existing omelette over the new layer. Repeat this process with the remaining egg mixture, creating multiple layers.

instructions.title: Finish and Slice
instructions.text: Once all the egg mixture is used and the omelette is fully rolled, gently cook it for another minute or two, turning it occasionally to ensure all sides are cooked through and slightly golden. Transfer the Gyeran Mari to a cutting board. Let it cool for a few minutes before slicing it into 1-inch thick pieces. This helps maintain its shape.

instructions.title: Serve Warm
instructions.text: Arrange the sliced Korean Rolled Omelette on a serving plate. Gyeran Mari is delicious served warm or at room temperature as a side dish (banchan), part of a lunchbox (dosirak), or a light breakfast. Enjoy with a sprinkle of sesame seeds or a drizzle of soy sauce if desired.

tips: Use a non-stick pan and maintain low heat to prevent browning and ensure even cooking for a soft, fluffy texture.
For variations, add finely minced ham, seaweed flakes (gim), or cheese to the egg mixture. Ensure add-ins are very small to prevent tearing while rolling.
Let the Gyeran Mari cool slightly on a cutting board before slicing. This helps it firm up and prevents it from falling apart, resulting in cleaner, more attractive slices.
article: Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette is a beloved Korean Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
